info.title: Home Test Supplier API
info.description:
## Overview

Use this API to send completed home test results and order status updates to the HomeTest service.

HomeTest is the API producer and test kit suppliers are the API consumers. Suppliers use this API after they have received an order through the companion supplier-hosted API and need to tell HomeTest what happened next.

You can:

* submit a completed test result
* update the status of a test order

You cannot use this API to:

* create a new test order
* check whether a person is eligible for a test
* retrieve patient demographic details
* retrieve historical test results

Payloads use [FHIR](https://digital.nhs.uk/developer/guides-and-documentation/our-api-technologies#fhir) resources encoded as `application/fhir+json`.

## Who can use this API

This API is intended for approved home test suppliers integrating with the HomeTest service.

Suppliers must agree their use case and onboarding route with the HomeTest team before using this API.

## Related APIs

The companion supplier-hosted API is used by HomeTest to send orders to suppliers and check order eligibility. This API is the reverse direction: suppliers use it to send results and order status updates back to HomeTest.

## API status and roadmap

This API is being prepared for private beta on the NHS API Platform.

The current documentation is for review and publication through Proxy Generator. A callable sandbox is not available yet.

For questions about the roadmap, use the [NHS Digital Developer Community](https://developer.community.nhs.uk/) or contact the HomeTest team.

## Service level

A service level has not been published yet.

## Technology

This API is a RESTful API using OpenAPI 3.0.3.

Request and response bodies use FHIR JSON resources. The FHIR resources are constrained by this API specification and the HomeTest integration guidance.

## Network access

The API is expected to be available over the internet through the NHS API Platform when deployed.

## Security and authorisation

This draft uses bearer token authorisation because suppliers are system-to-system API consumers.

The final access level must be confirmed with the NHS API Platform team before production use.

## Errors

This API uses standard HTTP status codes:

* `200` to `299` means the request succeeded
* `400` to `499` means the request failed because of a client error
* `500` to `599` means the request failed because of a server error

Error responses use FHIR `OperationOutcome` resources where possible.

## Open source

The API contract is maintained in the [HomeTest supplier integration framework repository](https://github.com/NHSDigital/hometest-supplier-integration-framework).

## Environments and testing

A sandbox is planned but has not been deployed yet.

The "Try this API" feature is disabled for this documentation-only publication until a sandbox endpoint is available.

## Onboarding

Contact the HomeTest team to agree onboarding, supplier responsibilities and access to test environments.

contact.name: NHS England - Digital Prevention Services Portfolio - Home Test Team

servers.description: Sandbox environment. This endpoint is planned but not deployed yet.

post.description:
## Overview

Submit a completed home test result to HomeTest.

Use this endpoint when a supplier has processed a test and needs to send the result back to HomeTest.

The request body is a FHIR `Bundle` containing:

* a `DiagnosticReport`
* an `Observation`
* a `Communication`

The `X-Correlation-ID` header is required for request tracking and idempotency.

## Business rules

The bundle must describe the result for an existing HomeTest order.

The `DiagnosticReport` groups the result resources. The `Observation` carries the coded test result. The `Communication` records whether clinical contact has been completed, is on hold, or is not needed.

## Responses

A successful request returns `201` with the accepted FHIR `Bundle`.

An invalid request returns `400` with a FHIR `OperationOutcome`.

An unauthorised request returns `401` with a FHIR `OperationOutcome`.

## Sandbox test scenarios

No sandbox is available yet. The "Try this API" feature is disabled for this documentation-only publication.

parameters.description: Unique identifier for request tracking and idempotency

post.description:
## Overview

Update the status of an existing HomeTest order.

Use this endpoint when a supplier needs to tell HomeTest that an order has moved to a new supplier-side state.

The request body is a FHIR `Task` resource. The FHIR `Task.status` value gives the standard FHIR task state. The `businessStatus.text` value gives the HomeTest-specific order state.

Expected HomeTest business status values are:

* `dispatched`
* `received-at-lab`
* `test-processed`

The `X-Correlation-ID` header is required for request tracking and idempotency.

## Business rules

The status update must refer to an existing HomeTest order.

Use `businessStatus.text` for the HomeTest-specific supplier state. Use `Task.status` for the standard FHIR task state.

## Responses

A successful request returns `200` with the accepted FHIR `Task`.

An invalid request returns `400` with a FHIR `OperationOutcome`.

A request for an unknown order returns `404` with a FHIR `OperationOutcome`.

## Sandbox test scenarios

No sandbox is available yet. The "Try this API" feature is disabled for this documentation-only publication.
